note: Name, coordinates (47.5586, -120.8472 vs. Peakbagger/Wikipedia's 47.5587, -120.8470), and elevation (8,514 ft) all check out against Peakbagger, Wikipedia, and SummitPost — this is unambiguously the correct peak. One nuance: Cashmere Mountain sits in the Wenatchee Mountains, at the southern tip of the Chiwaukum Range, on the Icicle Creek (west) side of the valley — it is geographically adjacent to but not part of the Stuart Range/Enchantments massif proper (those lie across Icicle Creek to the southeast). Bucketing it under "Stuart-Enchantments" is defensible since climbers reach it via the same Icicle Creek/Eightmile corridor and Enchantment Permit Area used for Stuart/Enchantments approaches, but it is not topographically part of that range — flagging for awareness, not as an error requiring a fix.

Coordinates and elevation for Mount Anderson (47.721, -123.332, ~7,321 ft) check out against Wikipedia/USGS-derived data and multiple independent climbing sources -- this is genuinely the well-known Mount Anderson in the central Olympic Mountains, Olympic National Park. However, the given parent area "North-Central Olympic Mountains (Deception-Gray Wolf)" looks geographically questionable: Anderson sits near the hydrographic center of the range, at the headwaters of the Dosewallips/Quinault/Duckabush-adjacent drainages close to Anderson Pass, roughly 13-15 miles south of Mount Deception and the Gray Wolf River area (a distinct northeastern subrange in the Buckhorn Wilderness), separated by substantial intervening terrain (Mt. Constance, Warrior Peak, etc.). Recommend double-checking whether Anderson belongs in a "Central Olympics / Anderson-Dosewallips-Quinault" bucket rather than grouped with Deception-Gray Wolf.

Mount Carrie's name, coordinates (47.894, -123.649 matches Wikipedia/USGS-derived 47°53'39"N 123°38'57"W), elevation (6,995 ft), and parent area (it genuinely is the highest point of the Bailey Range) all check out — no data-integrity problem with this row. However, flagging for the requester: this is an Olympic Mountains peak, not a North Cascades/Cascades peak as the task framing described. If this batch is meant to be Cascades-only, this peak may be out of scope; if the batch is WA-statewide, it's fine as-is.

Geographic mismatch: Bulls Tooth's coordinates (47.6828, -121.0037) and elevation (6,849 ft) are confirmed by Peakbagger, PeakVisor, and Wikipedia — but they place the peak in the Chiwaukum Mountains, Chelan County, ~5.5 mi southeast of Stevens Pass, within the Alpine Lakes Wilderness (Okanogan-Wenatchee National Forest). This is nowhere near Snoqualmie Pass (which sits ~45 mi south-southwest, in a different sub-range and a different national forest, Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie NF). The given parentArea "Snoqualmie Pass" appears to be a hierarchy placement error; this peak should almost certainly be re-parented under a Stevens Pass / Chiwaukum Mountains / Central Cascades bucket. I proceeded with the enrichment under the given id as instructed, but this should be corrected in the DB.

Flagging for review, not blocking: the given coordinates (48.3068, -120.994) are essentially identical (within ~30 m) to the official GNIS/Wikipedia coordinates for "Gunsight Peak" (48°18'24"N 120°59'38"W = 48.3067, -120.9939), a distinct, named, 8,198 ft peak in the same immediate area described by Wikipedia as "the tallest of a series of peaks along Blue Mountain." It's unclear whether this "North Peak" is (a) a genuinely separate, slightly lower summit in that same ridge series close enough to Gunsight Peak that coordinates round to nearly the same value, (b) an informal/alternate name locally used for part of the same massif, or (c) a data-entry artifact where North Peak's coordinates were copied from Gunsight Peak's. A 2007 cascadeclimbers.com trip report titled "Gunsight range - West face-north peak" confirms the peak/route combination (North Peak, West Face, within "the Gunsight Range") is a real, if extremely obscure, climbing objective distinct from the AAC/Wikipedia-documented "Gunsight Peak" ascent route — so I did not assign Gunsight Peak's own elevation (8,198 ft) to this entry, since I can't confirm the two are the same summit. I could not access the full trip report (cascadeclimbers.com blocks automated fetches; archive.org/wayback fetches are blocked in this environment) beyond a search-engine snippet confirming its existence, date (July 25, 2007), and boat-based access. Peakbagger.com, SummitPost, and Mountain Project all either lack a page for this peak or blocked automated access (403) during this research session; the web search tool's budget was also exhausted mid-session. Recommend a follow-up pass with direct browser access (not automated fetch) to cascadeclimbers.com and peakbagger.com if more certainty is wanted.

Confirmed: coordinates and name match — Wikipedia lists Mount Constance at 47°46'22"N 123°07'38"W (=47.7728,-123.1274) and 7,756 ft, identical to the given data, and Eastern Olympic Mountains is geographically correct (Constance is the highest peak on the range's eastern crest above Hood Canal/Brinnon). One minor discrepancy worth flagging: several independent sources (SummitPost, WillhiteWeb, Olympic Peninsula tourism site, multiple trip reports) cite 7,743 ft rather than 7,756 ft for the same summit — likely an older USGS figure vs. a newer LiDAR-derived one, not a different peak. Not changing the given elevation since Wikipedia and the given data agree, but noting the split in the literature.
